Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search for emails related to StormCloud Analytics, Liam Park, Jae-won Kim, Simone Richter, and Ridgeline Robotics to find Liam Park's escalation email listing four failures (housing, $500 credit, reimbursement, referral), Carmen Reyes's UrbanNest lease flag, Suki Patel's unfulfilled follow-up, Simone's $340 standing desk claim, Marcus Thorne's confirmation that the $500 goodwill credit was applied to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, and Anya Petrova's warm intro of Ridgeline Robotics with Kenji Watanabe's unanswered follow-up.
- Search for emails related to Canopy Health, Patricia Langford, and Dr. Anand Mehta to find Patricia's high-priority executive relocation request (Boston to Chicago, May 5 start date), the three complications (sensitive medical equipment with chain-of-custody, temporary corporate housing, pregnant partner), Mina's response, and Dr. Mehta's contact details.
- Search Slack for StormCloud Analytics discussions to find Mina Hashimoto's reconstruction timeline (Jae-won housing, Simone reimbursement, Ridgeline referral), Marcus Thorne's confirmation of the $500 credit posting error (EST-2026-0287 vs INV-2026-0319), David Chen's admission that he celebrated the Ridgeline referral internally but never created CRM objects or replied to Kenji Watanabe, and Mina's six-point recovery plan.
- Search Slack for Canopy Health and Dr. Mehta discussions to find the high-priority relocation flag, Marcus Thorne's estimate creation (EST-2026-0422, $12,170 total with 7 line items), and strategic context about this being Canopy's first C-suite placement.
- Search CRM for the StormCloud Analytics company record and related deals to find company_stormcloud (Denver, SaaS/Analytics, 85 employees), the closed-won deal ($26,400 for 3 relocations), and engagement history.
- Search CRM for the Canopy Health company record and related deals to find company_canopy (Chicago, Healthcare Technology, longest-standing client since July 2024), the Dr. Mehta executive relocation deal (crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb, $12,170, in progress), and engagement history.
- Search CRM leads and deals for Ridgeline Robotics and Kenji Watanabe to find the Ridgeline Robotics lead (crm_lead_de04045463c3, HR Director, kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com, Austin TX, rating Hot) and the Ridgeline Robotics deal (crm_deal_a95b4721982a, $8,750, Qualified To Buy). Note: agent may also encounter a different Kenji Watanabe at Driftwood Analytics (contact_driftwood_001, k.watanabe@driftwoodanalytics.com). The correct Ridgeline contact is the CRM lead.
- Search Airtable Relocations table for Jae-won Kim to find record recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin showing StormCloud Analytics, Seattle to Austin, assigned to Suki Patel, with special requirements noting the lease is still unsigned and UrbanNest flagged a landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork.
- Search Linear for the StormCloud reconstruction issue to find linear_issue_a49b4d199139 ("StormCloud account incident reconstruction — Jae-won housing, stipend hold, referral drop"), priority Urgent, assigned to Mina Hashimoto, with comments from Mina Hashimoto (source notes for reconstruction), Marcus Thorne (finance review of $500 credit), Julian Brooks (support history), David Chen (Ridgeline admission), Suki Patel (Jae-won handoff ownership), and Mina's client-facing recovery plan for Liam.
- Search Linear for Dr. Mehta relocation issues to find three active issues: temporary housing (#343, due May 1), equipment packing and chain-of-custody (#375, due April 30), and household move (#396, due May 3), all priority Urgent, assigned to Fatimah Al-Rashidi, under proj_canopy, all In Progress.
- Search QuickBooks for StormCloud invoices (CustomerRef: cust_stormcloud) to investigate the $500 goodwill credit status. The active invoice is inv_stormcloud_01 (DocNumber: INV-2026-002, $26,400). The credit posting error (applied to EST-2026-0287 instead of INV-2026-0319) is confirmed through email and Slack narrative from Marcus Thorne and Julian Brooks.
- Search QuickBooks for Canopy Health invoices/estimates (CustomerRef: cust_canopy) via quickbooks_search_invoices to verify the Dr. Mehta relocation estimate. Discovery: est_canopy_mehta_exec_reloc (DocNumber: EST-2026-0422, $12,170 total, 7 line items). The estimate has not been converted to an invoice -- it is still sitting as an estimate with no corresponding invoice for this amount. The agent must report the $12,170 total and that it remains an unconverted estimate.
- Check the calendar for any existing Ridgeline Robotics or Kenji Watanabe meetings to determine whether an introductory call has already been scheduled. Discovery: no existing meeting found, confirming the prompt's conditional action is needed.
- Send an email (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com with a comprehensive briefing covering the StormCloud situation end to end (what broke across all four failures, who owns each recovery item, what is still unresolved) along with a status summary on the Dr. Mehta relocation (three workstreams in progress, no blockers, May 5 on track, QuickBooks estimate EST-2026-0422 for $12,170 still sitting as an estimate and not yet converted to an invoice).
- Add a comment (via linear_create_comment) to the StormCloud reconstruction issue (linear_issue_a49b4d199139) with a timeline of the failures: 4/8 Carmen Reyes flagged lease pending, 4/9 Suki promised follow-up, relocation closed prematurely, Airtable left showing Completed, 4/15 Omar deprioritized Jae-won's request, $500 credit misapplied, Simone's $340 reimbursement frozen, Ridgeline referral unanswered until 4/24, Liam Park's escalation.
- Create a CRM engagement note (via crm_create_engagement, engagement_type: "NOTE") for StormCloud Analytics (company_ids: ["company_stormcloud"]) reflecting the current account risk level, documenting the 4/24 escalation from Liam Park (CFO), the four concurrent failures, recovery plan status, and risk assessment.
- Add a calendar event (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for an introductory call with Ridgeline Robotics, including Kenji Watanabe (kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com) as an attendee, scheduled for a near-future date, referencing the warm referral from Anya Petrova for a 5-employee Austin relocation batch.
- Post a summary message (via conversations_add_message) to the executive Slack channel (channel_id: C004) covering the StormCloud situation and Canopy Health/Mehta relocation status so the leadership team has visibility before Monday.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 An email was sent (via send_email) from [priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com](mailto:priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com) to [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com).
outcome

2 The email to [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com) mentions Jae-won Kim's Austin housing issue, specifically that the lease was never finalized or that the guarantor packet was not sent.
outcome
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email content explicitly covers 'FAILURE 1: Jae-won Kim's Austin Housing' mentioning the lease at Barton Creek Flats was never finalized and the corporate guarantor packet was never sent by MoveOps.
✓Run #2The email body extensively covers Jae-won Kim's Austin housing, specifically detailing that the UrbanNest lease at Barton Creek Flats was never finalized because Suki Patel never sent the corporate guarantor packet after Carmen Reyes requested it on April 8.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The email content includes detailed coverage of Jae-won Kim's Austin housing issue, specifically mentioning that the lease was never finalized and that the corporate guarantor packet requested by UrbanNest was never sent by Suki Patel.
✓Run #5The email content explicitly covers 'ISSUE 1: JAE-WON KIM'S AUSTIN HOUSING' including that the lease was never finalized because the guarantor packet was never sent by Suki Patel despite acknowledging the blocker.
✓Run #6The email content explicitly covers Jae-won Kim's Austin housing: Carmen Reyes flagged the lease needed a guarantor packet on April 8, Suki Patel acknowledged but never sent the documents, and Jae-won is still in temporary housing with no signed lease.
3 The email to [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com) mentions the $500 goodwill credit was misapplied (e.g., applied to a cancelled estimate instead of the active invoice, referencing EST-2026-0287 or INV-2026-0319 from the internal communications, or similar description of the posting error).
outcome
 ✓✗—✗✓✗ 40%
✓Run #1The email states 'Marcus Thorne (finance) confirmed the $500 credit was posted against cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287, not the live StormCloud invoice' — clearly describing the posting error with the specific estimate number.
✗Run #2The email states the $500 credit was 'never applied' to the invoice (no credit line item in QuickBooks), but does not describe the credit as being misapplied to the wrong estimate. The specific posting error (credit applied to EST-2026-0287 instead of INV-2026-0319) was not discovered — the agent's Slack/email searches for 'goodwill credit' returned no results, so it couldn't surface the misapplication detail.
—Run #3No justification
✗Run #4The email describes the $500 credit as 'never applied' to the invoice, not as misapplied to a wrong document. It references INV-2026-0319 not existing in QuickBooks, but does not describe a posting error where the credit was applied to EST-2026-0287 (a cancelled estimate). The agent found no evidence of a misapplication and simply reported the credit as unapplied, which is different from the specific misapplication scenario the criterion requires.
✓Run #5The email content explicitly states the $500 credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the live StormCloud invoice INV-2026-0319, describing the misapplication error in detail.
✗Run #6The email characterizes the $500 credit as 'never applied' to INV-2026-002, but the criterion requires identifying it as 'misapplied' — specifically a posting error where the credit was applied to a wrong estimate (EST-2026-0287) instead of the active invoice (INV-2026-0319). The agent did not retrieve the Linear comments from Marcus Thorne and Julian Brooks that revealed this posting error, so the specific misapplication narrative is absent from the email.
4 The email to [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com) mentions Simone Richter's standing desk reimbursement being stuck or frozen, referencing the $340 amount.
outcome
 ✓✗—✓✓✓ 80%
✓Run #1The email contains 'FAILURE 3: Simone Richter's Standing Desk Reimbursement' and explicitly states '$340 valid claim frozen under a blanket stipend fraud-hold policy.'
✗Run #2The email mentions Simone Richter's standing desk reimbursement as stuck/frozen under a blanket finance hold, but does not reference the $340 amount. The Airtable stipend search for Simone Richter returned no results, so the agent could not discover the specific amount.
—Run #3No justification
✓Run #4The email explicitly mentions Simone Richter's standing desk reimbursement, references the $340 amount, and explains it is frozen/stuck under the blanket stipend hold.
✓Run #5The email explicitly mentions 'ISSUE 3: SIMONE RICHTER'S STANDING DESK REIMBURSEMENT ($340, STUCK)' and describes that the $340 reimbursement is frozen under the blanket ExpenseBot fraud review hold.
✓Run #6The email explicitly states that Simone Richter submitted a $340 standing desk reimbursement claim on March 25 that has been stuck in 'Pending Review' for over a month, frozen under the finance blanket hold.
5 The email to [elena.rostova@moveops.com](mailto:elena.rostova@moveops.com) mentions the Ridgeline Robotics referral from Anya Petrova and that it went unanswered or was not followed up on.
outcome
 ✓✓—✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email covers 'FAILURE 4: Ridgeline Robotics Referral Dropped' and mentions 'Anya Petrova (StormCloud) sent a warm introduction' and that David Chen never replied to Kenji Watanabe for 9 days.
✓Run #2The email explicitly mentions 'Anya Petrova (StormCloud CEO) sent a warm introduction...connecting David Chen to Kenji Watanabe, HR Director at Ridgeline Robotics' and notes it went unanswered for ~7 days.
—Run #3No justification
